Custom Vinyl Window Graphics

Turn Glass Into a Canvas with Vinyl Window Graphics

ŌśģŌśģŌśģŌśģŌśģ

Read All Reviews
  • Order Process
  • Cut Options
  • Artwork
  • Shipping
  • Installation

Design Methods

Design from Scratch

With our easy to use online designer, you can quickly design your vinyl window graphic. Just upload an image or logo from your computer or choose from our wide selection of clipart to create your personalized window decal.

Upload a File

If you decide to upload an original file, instead of designing your own, you will need to upload an .eps file. This ensures that you will receive the highest quality window graphic possible! Once you have added your product to your cart and select your shipping method, a proof will be emailed to you. When you approve your proof and make a payment, your order will be complete.

Payment

All orders must be paid up front prior to being put into production. We accept VISA, Mastercard, American Express, and Discover credit cards. We also accept checks, which must be received prior to your decals being put into production.

Live Order Tracking

Back in the early days of making decals, we built a system called Live Order Tracking that enables our customers to track their order through every stage of our streamlined process, from order placement to delivery.

After placing your order for vinyl window graphics, you’ll receive a unique Live Order Tracking number via email that will allow you to track your window decals every step of the way. Need the current status of your order? Just click the link in the email and get the most recent updates from our dynamic Live Order Tracking system.

Die Cut, Kiss Cut, and Transfer Cut Decals

The main differences between these three cut options, comes down to the paper backing on each decal.

Die-Cut

Die-cut means that we are cutting through both the vinyl decal material and the paper backing. This cut type doesn't leave any material around the decal. Another way to look at it is to think of it like a "cookie cutter." Your decals are all individually cut out to the contour of your design, so you have individually cut decals with no paper material around the design.

* Please note – Back Slit (Crack and Peel) is only available on our Standard and Permanent material options *

Kiss-Cut

Kiss-cut means we are only cutting through the vinyl layer ("decal material") and not cutting through the paper backing, leaving a paper material border around your decal. When you choose a kiss-cut decal, you will have extra material around your decal for easy peeling.

Transfer-Cut

Transfer cut option means that we will be cutting the vinyl decal material to a specified shape, graphic or text and then removing or “picking” any excess or unwanted vinyl material from your design leaving no vinyl background on your decal. We then place a transfer tape over the top of the remaining pieces of vinyl. This tape is what holds all the individual cut pieces of your decal design once you remove the paper backing from your decal. It also allows you to place multiple individual pieces of vinyl decal material on your desired surface cleanly and evenly.

* Vector File (EPS or Ai – Adobe Illustrator) format needed for Transfer Cut decals. *

Transfer Cut / Pick Charge

Transfer cut price includes 8 standard picks with a Maximum of 40 picks per sq. foot.

Additional picks are $0.02 to $0.04 per pick depending on difficulty/intricacy.

Intricately cut decals will be custom quoted based on difficulty and time required to pick.

For more information visit our transfer cut guidelines.

Vinyl Window Graphic Artwork Specifications

Preferred Files for Printing

The maximum file size we accept is 100 MB. Our preferred file types are:

  • Adobe Illustrator EPS (Encapsulated Post Script)
  • PDF (Adobe PDF)

When submitting these files, make sure you convert all fonts to outlines or curves to ensure they print correctly and the fonts are not substituted. 

Additional Files Accepted

  • JPG (High Resolution JPEG)
  • TIFF (High Resolution TIFF)
  • PNG (High Resolution PNG)

File Tips

  • Set up size proportionate to the exact size product you are ordering 
  • Convert all text to outlines 
  • Only Allow for bleed if necessary
  • Never flatten/merge layers in a PSD file
  • Indicate the cut line clearly for all decal products

Transfer-Cut Guidelines

Transfer Cutting refers to any sticker or decal that will be picked or weeded with transfer tape applied over the top of it. Most commonly used for vinyl lettering.

  • Simple and open fonts are recommended.
  • All text must be at least 1/2" tall.
  • The space between characters and other design elements must be at least 1/8”.
  • Main strokes, line weights, or art elements should be a minimum of 1/8”
  • Minimum pick size of 1/8”.
  • We recommend you request a proof for this product.

Disclaimers:

  • Transfer cut price includes 8 standard picks with a Maximum of 40 picks per sq. foot.
  • Additional picks are $0.02 to $0.04 per pick depending on difficulty/intricacy.
  • Intricately cut decals will be custom quoted based on difficulty and time required to pick.

Vector Art

Vector art consists of lines and curves that are mathematically defined objects. When viewing art in key line mode you can see the points or vectors when connecting your lines and curves. They can also be seen when using the pen tool or editing the points along a path. Vector art is ideal for type and drawn shapes because they can be enlarged to any size while maintaining crisp outlines and details without sacrificing quality. The distance between the vectors is calculated mathematically and is not dependent upon resolution. 

Raster Art

Raster images consist of colored squares, called pixels. They are created by combining a series of various colored pixels. Digital photographs are made up of pixels. If you view a raster image at 200% or more you are able to see the individual pixels that make up the image. Raster images are dependent upon resolution so image enlargement is limited by the individual size at which the file was created. If a file is produced at a size that is larger than its resolution will allow, the quality will decrease. Printing a low resolution file will result in pixelation. Individual pixels are large enough to be easily discernable. Increasing the resolution will not solve this problem is there are not enough pixels to begin with. The program will add more pixels based upon the estimation of their location and will then assign their neighboring pixels's color value. This may cause an image to look muddy. 

If submitting these files, please follow the guidelines below:

  • 100% scale (full size output) at 100 dpi
  • 50% scale at 200 dpi or higher
  • 25% scale at 300 dpi or higher
  • 10% scale at 600-1200 dpi recommended

Design Services

If you are not able to provide us with a good quality image, we do have a vectorizing/recreating service available. Most logos or images can be recreated for as little as $15; however images with more detail are subject to quoting prior to vectorizing the image. Please contact a sales representative for more details.

This service needs to be paid for in advance of us printing your order. We will keep your recreated vector artwork on file for you to use on all future orders.

Releasing the recreated vector artwork to you for personal use can be done for an additional cost.

 

Shipping

  • You can expect your Decals.com order to arrive within 2 - 4 business days (after production time) to 90% of the United States.
  • Unless otherwise specified, at the time of ordering, your vinyl window graphics will ship via UPS Ground.
  • If you have any questions about shipping rates or rush production, please contact our friendly Customer Service Representatives at (320) 391-5250 prior to ordering.
  • You will receive a tracking number once your package has shipped.
  • To read more about our shipping information visit our shipping policy page.

Shipping Methods:

  • Economy (5-15 Days)
  • Standard (3-5 Business Days)
  • Three Day (3 Business Days)
  • Two Day (2 Business Days)
  • Overnight (1 Business Day)

Important Economy Shipping Notes:

  • If choosing economy shipping, please include additional postal information such as your P.O. Box in the "Address Two" field on the checkout page to help ensure delivery.
  • Using economy shipping can also add an additional 5 - 10 days, depending on where the package is being shipped to. 

National and International Shipping Notes:

  • Decals.com ships throughout the United States and Internationally.
  • All international shipments must have shipping labels as well as customs documentation provided by the customers. We will only accept international shipping labels from the following carriers, USPS and UPS.
  • Contact a Customer Service Representative at (320) 391-5250 for more information.

How to Apply Vinyl Decals

Decals are a great way to add a unique touch to many spaces and surfaces. Whether you plan to display your new decal on your car, laptop, living-room wall, or anywhere else, proper installation is crucial for creating a strong bond that lasts long and looks great. That's why we've put together this guide that teaches you how to apply decals in just six easy steps.

Watch Die-cut Video Installation Guide
Watch Transfer Decal Video Installation Guide

Here’s the thing about windows: they’re everywhere, but most of the time they’re just… there. With vinyl window graphics, you can make them work for you—whether it’s branding your storefront, decorating an office door, or adding flair to your car. These are printed on the same tough vinyl as our custom decals, so they’ll hold up outdoors but still go on smooth thanks to air-release backing.

You get to call the shots. Glossy or matte? No problem. Want your design cut to shape? Pick die cut or kiss cut. Want that sleek, no-background look so it feels painted on? That’s where the transfer cut shines.

      Why People Love Vinyl Window Graphics Return to Top

      Windows are prime space, and with graphics you can:

      • Put business hours or your logo front and center where customers see it
      • Run seasonal promos without committing to permanent signage
      • Brand fleet vehicles so they advertise while they drive
      • Add privacy, style, or both to office glass

      The nice part? They’re UV-protected and weather-resistant, so they won’t fade after a few weeks in the sun.

      Design Tips That Actually Help

      Want a polished look without overthinking it? A couple of quick ideas:

      • Use white lettering or bright colors if your windows are tinted
      • Size designs to fit natural frames (like panes or door panels)
      • Go with transfer cut if you want just the lettering and no clear border
      • Layer them with window clings for short-term or seasonal updates

      These small choices can make a big difference once the decal is up.

       

      4 Key Benefits — Custom Window Graphics for Stores & Offices
      1. Premium vinyl materials for stellar quality
      2. Custom cut for unique logos and shapes
      3. Weather & water resistant for increased lifespan
      4. Damage and residue free removal

      Expand your creativity beyond the windows! Explore our decorative wall decals – perfect for enhancing both commercial spaces and personal havens. Don't miss out, transform your space today!


      Customer Reviews ... Reviews

      ŌśģŌśģŌśģŌśģŌśģ

      Don't take our word for it! See what others are saying.

      See Our Reviews More Reviews

      Why Choose Decals.com?

      Customer Service

      Our customer service is 100% in-house and comprised of friendly, professional graphic designers.

      Made in the USA

      All of our products are proudly made and manufactured here in the USA!

       

      Fast Turnaround

      Standard turnaround time ships your product within three business days.

      Track Your Order

      Track your order, every step of the way, with our proprietary live order tracking system.

      Frequently Asked Questions Return to Top

      Product Information

      What surfaces can vinyl window graphics be placed on?

      Vinyl Window Graphics are intended for use on windows and other glass surfaces. See our custom wall decals product for even more applications!

      Will the decal leave any sticky residue behind?

      We use a removable adhesive on our window decals so they can be removed cleanly. If some residue is left behind, use a light cleaning solution to remove it.

      What are the differences between window stickers, perforated window vinyl and vinyl window decals?

      In general, window stickers are what dealerships place in a car window to display specs and information about the vehicle. They do this so customers (or potential ones) roaming the parking lot looking at cars get a better understanding of base price and features.
      Window perfs or perforated window vinyl is what you would see on the large storefront windows of a shopping center. Big box stores also use these so they can display branding and sales that will get noticed form the interstate or frontage roads.
      Our vinyl window decals, or vinyl graphics, are like stickers, technically every decal is a sticker, but their purpose is slightly different. The common use for our vinyl decals is smaller messaging or graphics on storefront windows or just for basic custom window decals.

      What material is used for your vinyl window graphics?

      We offer two vinyl material options for our vinyl window graphics. Our standard vinyl material is made from calendered vinyl that has a three to five-year outdoor durability lifespan when properly applied and maintained.

      If you are looking for something more durable we recommend our hi-performance vinyl which is made from a cast vinyl material that has up to an 8 year outdoor durability lifespan when properly applied and maintained. This material features air release technology for smoother application.

      Can I use multiple colors, fonts and images in my design?

      Yes you can, because we digitally print all our decals, you can use as many fonts, colors, and images as you want at no extra charge.

      The only time color will affect your price is if you request a color match proof. If you need a color match proof, please contact us to speak with one of our friendly customer service reps!

      Do your decals have a glossy or matte finish?

      Our vinyl window graphics are available in either a gloss or matte finish.

      Are your custom vinyl window graphics repositionable?

      We do not recommend trying to reposition our decals after installation. While they will remove cleanly if you take them off, reapplying them will likely be unsuccessful since the adhesive is only designed for single installations.

      If you are looking for a removable window decal option, we suggest you order our custom window cling product. This product is printed on a low tack adhesive window cling material that allows it to be removed and applied multiple times.

      How large can my decal be?

      Our vinyl window graphics can be printed up to 120” wide and 52” high. You can create larger displays by combining multiple decal panels together. Please contact us if you need an extra large decal and we will guide you through the process.

      Will my design impact the pricing of my decal?

      Colors, fonts, and images will not affect the pricing of your window graphic. The only factors that will impact the price of your decal are the size and quantity you order and whether you request a color match proof. If you need a color match proof, please contact us to speak with one of our friendly customer service reps!

      Do they go inside or outside the window?

      These vinyl window graphics get applied to the outside of the window, but both options exist. Inside-glass keeps them safe from the weather; outside-glass makes them visible in more conditions. Check out our clear decals if you would like to apply your decal to the inside of the glass.

      Can I take them off later?

      Yes. They’re made for long-term use but can be removed carefully.

      Will I fight bubbles during install?

      Nope. The vinyl has air release tech built in. Smooth it down and you’re good. If you do come across a bubble, check out our helpful video on how to remove an air bubble from under your sticker.

      What is the lifespan of your vinyl window graphics?

      Material Lifespan (when properly applied & maintained)
      Standard Vinyl 3-5 years
      High Performance Vinyl Up to 8 years

      Common Uses

      What are some common uses for custom window graphics?

      Window graphics are commonly used for storefronts, office windows, and glass doors. Here are some common uses:

      • Custom business logo decals
      • Displaying company contact information such as phone numbers and business hours
      • Product and service advertisements on storefront or office windows
      • Event announcements and promotion

      Shop Vinyl Window Graphics

      Glass doesn’t have to stay plain. With vinyl window graphics, your space gets a custom, professional upgrade that lasts. Shop window graphics here or explore related products like business logo decals and transfer decals to complete your branding.

Just upload an image or logo from your computer or choose from our wide selection of clipart to create your personalized window decal. Upload a File If you decide to upload an original file, instead of designing your own, you will need to upload an .eps file. This ensures that you will receive the highest quality window graphic possible! Once you have added your product to your cart and select your shipping method, a proof will be emailed to you. When you approve your proof and make a payment, your order will be complete. Payment All orders must be paid up front prior to being put into production. We accept VISA, Mastercard, American Express, and Discover credit cards. We also accept checks, which must be received prior to your decals being put into production. Live Order Tracking Back in the early days of making decals, we built a system called Live Order Tracking that enables our customers to track their order through every stage of our streamlined process, from order placement to delivery. After placing your order for vinyl window graphics, youŌĆÖll receive a unique Live Order Tracking number via email that will allow you to track your window decals every step of the way. Need the current status of your order? Just click the link in the email and get the most recent updates from our dynamic Live Order Tracking system. Die Cut, Kiss Cut, and Transfer Cut Decals The main differences between these three cut options, comes down to the paper backing on each decal. Die-Cut Die-cut means that we are cutting through both the vinyl decal material and the paper backing. This cut type doesn't leave any material around the decal. Another way to look at it is to think of it like a "cookie cutter." Your decals are all individually cut out to the contour of your design, so you have individually cut decals with no paper material around the design. * Please note ŌĆō Back Slit (Crack and Peel) is only available on our Standard and Permanent material options * Kiss-Cut Kiss-cut means we are only cutting through the vinyl layer ("decal material") and not cutting through the paper backing, leaving a paper material border around your decal. When you choose a kiss-cut decal, you will have extra material around your decal for easy peeling. Transfer-Cut Transfer cut option means that we will be cutting the vinyl decal material to a specified shape, graphic or text and then removing or ŌĆ£pickingŌĆØ any excess or unwanted vinyl material from your design leaving no vinyl background on your decal. We then place a transfer tape over the top of the remaining pieces of vinyl. This tape is what holds all the individual cut pieces of your decal design once you remove the paper backing from your decal. It also allows you to place multiple individual pieces of vinyl decal material on your desired surface cleanly and evenly. * Vector File (EPS or Ai ŌĆō Adobe Illustrator) format needed for Transfer Cut decals. * Transfer Cut / Pick Charge Transfer cut price includes 8 standard picks with a Maximum of 40 picks per sq. foot. Additional picks are $0.02 to $0.04 per pick depending on difficulty/intricacy. Intricately cut decals will be custom quoted based on difficulty and time required to pick. For more information visit our transfer cut guidelines. Vinyl Window Graphic Artwork Specifications Preferred Files for Printing The maximum file size we accept is 100 MB. Our preferred file types are: Adobe Illustrator EPS (Encapsulated Post Script) PDF (Adobe PDF) When submitting these files, make sure you convert all fonts to outlines or curves to ensure they print correctly and the fonts are not substituted. Additional Files Accepted JPG (High Resolution JPEG) TIFF (High Resolution TIFF) PNG (High Resolution PNG) File Tips Set up size proportionate to the exact size product you are ordering Convert all text to outlines Only Allow for bleed if necessary Never flatten/merge layers in a PSD file Indicate the cut line clearly for all decal products Transfer-Cut Guidelines Transfer Cutting refers to any sticker or decal that will be picked or weeded with transfer tape applied over the top of it. Most commonly used for vinyl lettering. Simple and open fonts are recommended. All text must be at least 1/2" tall. The space between characters and other design elements must be at least 1/8ŌĆØ. Main strokes, line weights, or art elements should be a minimum of 1/8ŌĆØ Minimum pick size of 1/8ŌĆØ. We recommend you request a proof for this product. Disclaimers: Transfer cut price includes 8 standard picks with a Maximum of 40 picks per sq. foot. Additional picks are $0.02 to $0.04 per pick depending on difficulty/intricacy. Intricately cut decals will be custom quoted based on difficulty and time required to pick. Vector Art Vector art consists of lines and curves that are mathematically defined objects. When viewing art in key line mode you can see the points or vectors when connecting your lines and curves. They can also be seen when using the pen tool or editing the points along a path. Vector art is ideal for type and drawn shapes because they can be enlarged to any size while maintaining crisp outlines and details without sacrificing quality. The distance between the vectors is calculated mathematically and is not dependent upon resolution. Raster Art Raster images consist of colored squares, called pixels. They are created by combining a series of various colored pixels. Digital photographs are made up of pixels. If you view a raster image at 200% or more you are able to see the individual pixels that make up the image. Raster images are dependent upon resolution so image enlargement is limited by the individual size at which the file was created. If a file is produced at a size that is larger than its resolution will allow, the quality will decrease. Printing a low resolution file will result in pixelation. Individual pixels are large enough to be easily discernable. Increasing the resolution will not solve this problem is there are not enough pixels to begin with. The program will add more pixels based upon the estimation of their location and will then assign their neighboring pixels's color value. This may cause an image to look muddy. If submitting these files, please follow the guidelines below: 100% scale (full size output) at 100 dpi 50% scale at 200 dpi or higher 25% scale at 300 dpi or higher 10% scale at 600-1200 dpi recommended Design Services If you are not able to provide us with a good quality image, we do have a vectorizing/recreating service available. Most logos or images can be recreated for as little as $15; however images with more detail are subject to quoting prior to vectorizing the image. Please contact a sales representative for more details. This service needs to be paid for in advance of us printing your order. We will keep your recreated vector artwork on file for you to use on all future orders. Releasing the recreated vector artwork to you for personal use can be done for an additional cost. Shipping You can expect your Decals.com order to arrive within 2 - 4 business days (after production time) to 90% of the United States.
